metadata.title: High School Graduate AI Standards
metadata.description: Comprehensive academic and cognitive standards for high school graduate-level AI

mathematics.title: Linear Equations and Inequalities
mathematics.description: Solve linear equations and inequalities in one variable and interpret solutions in context
sample_questions: Solve for x: 3x + 5 = 2x - 7
A cell phone plan costs $40 per month plus $0.05 per text. Write an equation for monthly cost.
Graph the inequality: y ≥ 2x - 3

mathematics.title: Geometric Reasoning and Proof
mathematics.description: Use geometric theorems and properties to solve problems and write proofs
sample_questions: Prove that the angles in a triangle sum to 180 degrees
Find the area of a circle with radius 5 units
Determine if two triangles are congruent

mathematics.title: Introduction to Calculus Concepts
mathematics.description: Understand basic concepts of limits, derivatives, and integrals
sample_questions: What does the derivative represent?
Find the derivative of f(x) = x²
Estimate the area under a curve using rectangles
performance_indicators: Explains the concept of a limit
Interprets derivatives as rates of change

science.title: Forces and Motion
science.description: Analyze and predict motion using Newton's laws and kinematic equations
sample_questions: A car accelerates from 0 to 60 mph in 8 seconds. What is its acceleration?
Explain why you feel pushed back in your seat when a car accelerates
Calculate the kinetic energy of a 1000 kg car moving at 25 m/s
performance_indicators: Solves motion problems using kinematic equations
Applies Newton's laws to explain phenomena

science.title: Chemical Reactions and Stoichiometry
science.description: Balance chemical equations and perform stoichiometric calculations
sample_questions: Balance the equation: H₂ + O₂ → H₂O
How many grams of water are produced from 2 moles of hydrogen?
Identify the limiting reactant in a given reaction

science.title: Cell Structure and Function
science.description: Explain the relationship between cell structure and function in living organisms
sample_questions: What is the function of mitochondria in cells?
Explain how DNA codes for proteins
Compare plant and animal cell structures
performance_indicators: Identifies major cellular organelles and their functions
Explains the central dogma of molecular biology

english_language_arts.title: Reading Comprehension and Analysis
english_language_arts.description: Analyze complex texts for main ideas, themes, and literary devices
sample_questions: What is the central theme of this passage?
How does the author use symbolism to convey meaning?
Analyze the character development throughout the story
performance_indicators: Identifies main ideas and supporting details
Analyzes literary devices and their effects
Makes inferences based on textual evidence

english_language_arts.title: Argumentative and Informative Writing
english_language_arts.description: Write clear, coherent arguments and informative texts with proper evidence
sample_questions: Write a thesis statement for an essay about climate change
Develop a counterargument to your main position
Organize evidence to support your claims

social_studies.title: Historical Analysis and Interpretation
social_studies.description: Analyze historical events, causes, and effects using primary and secondary sources
sample_questions: What were the main causes of World War I?
How did the Industrial Revolution change society?
Compare different historical perspectives on this event

social_studies.title: Civic Ideals and Practices
social_studies.description: Understand democratic principles and civic responsibilities
sample_questions: What are the key principles of democratic government?
How do the three branches of government check each other?
What are your rights and responsibilities as a citizen?
performance_indicators: Analyzes the structure and function of government

communication_standards.description: Speak clearly and effectively in various contexts
examples: Give a 5-minute presentation on a chosen topic
assessment_criteria: Speaks clearly with appropriate volume and pace
Maintains eye contact and good posture

communication_standards.description: Write clearly and coherently for different purposes and audiences
examples: Write a persuasive essay with proper structure
assessment_criteria: Uses proper grammar, spelling, and punctuation
Organizes ideas logically with clear transitions
Adapts tone and style to audience and purpose

communication_standards.description: Communicate effectively using digital tools and platforms
assessment_criteria: Protects personal and others' privacy online

critical_thinking_skills: Solve complex problems using systematic approaches
Generate creative solutions to open-ended problems
Reflect on own learning and thinking processes
digital_literacy_standards: Navigate and search digital resources effectively
Evaluate the credibility of online information
Use productivity software for various tasks
Understand basic programming and computational thinking
Create digital content using various tools
